GitHub Action for my Nuxt.js blog hosted on GitHub Pages
GitHub Actions for a Nuxt.js blog hosted on GitHub Pages
This article was published in July 2021 and references Nuxt 2, Node.js 14, and older GitHub Action versions. While the core workflow concepts remain valid, specific action versions (e.g.,
) and Node.js versions have been updated since publication. Check official documentation for current best practices when implementing similar workflows today.

To update my blog, I usually build locally and then push changes to GitHub. This makes my git log unreadable because every deployment commit is mixed in with feature commits. This article shows how to use a GitHub Action to automate the deployment of my Nuxt.js blog to GitHub Pages, keeping the git history clean while ensuring continuous integration.
In this GitHub repo, the following workflow is triggered on pushes to the
for newer repos) which will build and deploy changes to
language-yaml shiki shiki-themes github-light github-dark monokai

name: github pages

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master
  pull_request:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: "14"

      - name: Cache dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - run: yarn
      - run: yarn lint
      - run: yarn generate

      - name: deploy
        uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./docs
